across.clue: To disturb someone means to upset that person.
To approve of something means you like or agree with that thing.
to put a load onto or into something such as a vehicle or container.
a man whose job is to look after the passengers on a plane train or ship especially serving them with food and drink.
A gentleman is a nice man.
To earn means to get money for the work you do.
the place where someone or something is going.
To grip something is to hold it very tightly.
An allowance is an amount of money given regularly.

down.clue: To make yourself look tidy and clean; to improve the appearance of something. | ___ up
To get information on a particular subject by reading a lot about it. | ___ up (on/about)
Laughter is the sound produced by laughing about something funny.

an arrangement in which you regularly pay an insurance company or other organisation an amount of money so that they will give you money if something you own is damaged lost or stolen or if you die or are ill or injured.
The ways in which a company encourages people to buy its products by deciding on price type of customer and advertising policy.
Friendship is the relationship between people who are friends.
If something is particular, then it is a single, important part of a group of things.
A collection is a group of things that have been gathered together.
To represent is to speak or act for a person or group.
When something is negative, it is unpleasant or sad.
